summary.why: Isolated lockfile-only commits (manual conflict resolution, auto-rebase byproducts) carry zero design intent — and the file-level filter keeps them pending whenever they're paired with a manifest change, so adding them to defaults is structurally safe.
summary.how: Added six suffix patterns (*package-lock.json, *pnpm-lock.yaml, *yarn.lock, *go.sum, *Cargo.lock, *Gemfile.lock) to defaultSkipPatterns; the existing skipSuffix matcher handles them without any matcher changes; tests cover both the lockfile cases and the manifest cases that must stay pending (package.json, go.mod, Cargo.toml).

notes: Surveyed osprey-strike commits to ground the defaults in real-world friction patterns. Considered the long tail (poetry.lock, Pipfile.lock, composer.lock, mix.lock, pubspec.lock) but the six included cover the bulk of agent-driven repos; the long tail can opt in via .timbersignore. Reviewer-style concern: a lockfile change unaccompanied by a manifest change CAN be substantive (manual transitive override, security patch). Accepted that risk because (a) the file-level filter only triggers on lockfile-ONLY commits, which are rare in practice, and (b) the timbers status visibility surface (infra_skipped_since_entry) catches drift.
